judgments.comparative_verdict: Both reports hit all four expectations with equivalent evidence; Report 2 is slightly cleaner on the AI-attribution flag while Report 1 is more thorough overall — effectively comparable.

report_1.evidence: "Stronger account security — Two-factor authentication is now enabled for every clinic by default." — **False; directly contradicted by code.** `app/security/config.py:3-4` reads `# Two-factor authentication is opt-in per clinic.` and `TWO_FACTOR_DEFAULT = False`... "Export your visit history — Patients can now export their full visit history as a PDF." — **False; feature not implemented.** `app/routers/visits.py` contains only `list_visits(...)` returning `[]` and the comment `# TODO: pdf export`... Remove this entire section.

report_2.evidence: `app/security/config.py` sets `TWO_FACTOR_DEFAULT = False` with the comment "Two-factor authentication is opt-in per clinic."... **Must be removed or corrected.**... `app/routers/visits.py` has only `list_visits()` returning `[]` and the literal marker `# TODO: pdf export`. There is no PDF export code... **Must be removed.**

report_1.evidence: `captures.json` corroborates this: `before_scheduling.png` → `"route": "/schedule"`, but `after_scheduling.png` → `"route": "/billing/invoices"`. So the pair the doc presents as "before vs. redesigned scheduling calendar" actually shows the scheduling page next to the **billing/invoices** page.

report_2.evidence: The "after" image, `after_scheduling.png`, is not the scheduling calendar at all — `captures.json` records its route as `/billing/invoices`, and the image itself literally reads **"BILLING / INVOICES"**... actually pairs the old calendar against an unrelated **billing/invoices screen**, mislabeled as the new calendar.

report_1.evidence: **`after_export.png` is referenced but does not exist** (line 26 of the doc... It is absent from the directory... **`unused_capture_07.png` exists but is referenced nowhere.** Opened, it shows **"PATIENT LIST"**... It is an orphan capture that should not be shipped.

report_2.evidence: **`after_export.png` — referenced (line 26, "Export your visit history") but the file does not exist.** Broken image link... **`unused_capture_07.png` — exists in the repo but is referenced nowhere** in the draft. It's an orphan capture reading "PATIENT LIST"

report_1.evidence: **The footer "Highlights draft assembled with AI assistance." (line 33).** Two issues for a customer send: it labels the piece a **draft**... and it carries AI-assistance attribution metadata.

report_2.evidence: **Footer: "Highlights draft assembled with AI assistance." (line 33)** — an internal drafting note; it should not be in a message sent to customers.
